mission.title: Install and Configure KubeAI
mission.description: KubeAI is an AI Inference Operator for Kubernetes designed to serve machine learning models, including LLMs and embeddings, in production environments. It simplifies the deployment and scaling of AI models while optimizing performance through intelligent load balancing. Key trade-offs include the need for sufficient hardware resources and the absence of dependencies on other systems like Istio or Knative.

steps.title: Add the KubeAI Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the KubeAI Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install KubeAI easily.

```bash

helm repo add kubeai https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/releases/download/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the correct Helm repository for KubeAI.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install KubeAI CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for KubeAI to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/releases/download/helm-chart-kubeai-0.23.1/kubeai-crds.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs necessary for KubeAI.

steps.title: Install KubeAI using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs KubeAI in the specified namespace with the desired version.

```bash

helm install kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --create-namespace --version 0.23.1

```

> Installs KubeAI in the kubeai namespace.

steps.title: Verify KubeAI installation
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the KubeAI pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> This command lists all pods in the kubeai namespace to verify their status.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a machine learning model using KubeAI.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/substratusai/kubeai/examples/example-model.yaml -n kubeai

```

> This command deploys an example model using KubeAI.

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is ready for use.

```bash

kubectl get kubeaimodels -n kubeai

```

> This command lists all KubeAI models to verify their readiness.

uninstall.title: Uninstall KubeAI
uninstall.description:
This step removes KubeAI and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all models and configurations associated with KubeAI.

```bash

helm uninstall kubeai --namespace kubeai

kubectl delete namespace kubeai

```

> This command uninstalls KubeAI from the kubeai namespace.
> This command deletes the kubeai namespace, which may leave orphaned resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade KubeAI
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step involves upgrading KubeAI to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kubeai -o wide

helm upgrade kubeai kubeai/kubeai --namespace kubeai --version 0.23.1

kubectl get pods -n kubeai

```

> Check the current status of the pods before upgrading.
> This command upgrades KubeAI to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KubeAI model not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified model does not exist or was not created successfully.

**Fix:**
Check the model definition and ensure it was applied correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on KubeAI pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI pods are failing to start due to configuration issues.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kubeai' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the KubeAI service'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The KubeAI service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Verify the service status with 'kubectl get svc -n kubeai' and check for any misconfigurations.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the KubeAI pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster meets the resource requirements and consider scaling your nodes.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved load balancing algorithms.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KubeAI pods running with a status of 'Running' when executing 'kubectl get pods -n kubeai'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
